```{r mi_analysis}
# Manual implementation of Rubin's rules for MI pooling
# Manual Rubin's rules pooling — replace the entire pool_results tryCatch block
manual_pool_mi <- function(estimates, ses, n_obs) {
  
  estimates <- unlist(estimates)
  ses <- unlist(ses)
  
  # Remove invalid entries
  valid <- is.finite(estimates) & is.finite(ses) & ses > 0
  estimates <- estimates[valid]
  ses <- ses[valid]
  m <- length(estimates)
  
  if(m < 2) stop("Need at least 2 valid imputations to pool")
  
  # Rubin's rules
  qbar <- mean(estimates)                          # pooled estimate
  Ubar <- mean(ses^2)                              # within-imputation variance
  B <- var(estimates)                           # between-imputation variance
  T_var <- Ubar + (1 + 1/m) * B                    # total variance
  se    <- sqrt(T_var)                              # pooled SE
  
  # Degrees of freedom (Barnard & Rubin 1999)
  lambda <- (1 + 1/m) * B / T_var                  # fraction of missing info
  df_old <- (m - 1) / lambda^2
  df_obs <- (n_obs - length(pred_vars) + 1) / 
            (n_obs - length(pred_vars) + 3) * 
            (n_obs - length(pred_vars)) * (1 - lambda)
  df <- df_old * df_obs / (df_old + df_obs)
  
  # CI and p-value
  t_crit <- qt(0.975, df = df)
  ci_lower <- qbar - t_crit * se
  ci_upper <- qbar + t_crit * se
  pval <- 2 * pt(abs(qbar / se), df = df, lower.tail = FALSE)
  
  list(
    qbar = qbar,
    sev= se,
    ci_lower = ci_lower,
    ci_upper = ci_upper,
    pval = pval,
    df = df,
    lambda = lambda,
    m = m
  )
}
```

Here we need to look at the overall simulation framework. This simulation has three layers to consider:

Layer 1 - Data Generating Conditions

For our dataset we need to consider what conditions are we going to be simulating under. This means sample sizes and hazard ratios (for both overall survival and disease free survival).

Layer 2 - Missingness Conditions (what kind of missing data are we looking at)
This includes:
 - Which variables have missingness
 - Proportion missing (0%, 25%, 50%)
 - Mechanism (MCAR, MAR, MNAR)
 - Mechanism-specific parameters (drivers for MAR, direction for MNAR)
 
 Layer 3 — Analysis methods (how we handle the missing data and estimate the treatment effect)

 - Complete case analysis
 - Multiple imputation
 - IPTW, matching, ebal — each applied to each dataset
 
 Due to the high number of variables to consider missginess it might be best pick 2-3 meaningful pairs rather than exhaust all possible combinations. 
 When it comes to MCAR data there are no extra stipulations we need. For MAR data we need to look at potential drivers, and the different strengths (how strongly does this driver predict missingness). Similarly for MNAR we need to consider the strength and direction of missing data. 

The following is the Analysis function - it applies the causal method onto a given dataset and applies the MI. 
```{r updated_analysis}
Analyze_WithEmbeddedBalance <- function(condition, dat, fixed_objects = NULL) {
  cat("CLASS OF DAT:", class(dat), "| IS DF:", is.data.frame(dat), "| NAMES:", paste(names(dat), collapse=","), "\n")
  suppressPackageStartupMessages({
    library(survival)
    library(MASS)
    library(WeightIt)
    library(ebal)
    library(mice)
    library(dplyr)
  })
  
  if(is.null(condition) || is.null(dat) || is.null(fixed_objects))
    stop("ANALYZE ERROR: NULL inputs detected")
  
  pred_vars<- fixed_objects$pred.vars.processed
  cat_vars <- fixed_objects$cat.vars.processed
  cont_vars <- fixed_objects$cont.vars.processed
  causal_method <- condition$causal_method
  missing_method <- condition$missing_method
  in.mis.subj <- condition$input_mis_subj
  
  # Helper: run one analysis on one dataset 
  run_analysis <- function(dataset, label, force_complete_case = FALSE) {
    
    if(force_complete_case || missing_method == "complete_case" || in.mis.subj == 0) {
      # complete case analysis
      att.dfs <- ff.att.analysis(dataset, 
                                 outcome_type = "DFS",
                                 method = causal_method,
                                 pred_vars = pred_vars,
                                 include_balance = FALSE,
                                 include_diagnostics = FALSE)
      att.os  <- ff.att.analysis(dataset, 
                                 outcome_type = "OS",
                                 method = causal_method,
                                 pred_vars = pred_vars,
                                 include_balance = FALSE,
                                 include_diagnostics = FALSE)
      
    } else if(missing_method == "multiple_imputation") {
      # MI analysis we set iterations to 5 for now
      nSim <- 5
      
      mi_results <- enhanced_mi_analysis(
        dataset = dataset,
        pred_vars = pred_vars,
        cat_vars = cat_vars,
        cont_vars = cont_vars,
        causal_method = causal_method,
        in.mis.subj = in.mis.subj,
        nSim = nSim,
        sim_seed = as.integer(Sys.time()) + sample(1e6, 1)
      )
      
      if(is.null(mi_results)) {
        att.dfs <- ff.att.analysis(dataset, 
                                   outcome_type = "DFS",
                                   method = causal_method,
                                   pred_vars = pred_vars,
                                   include_balance = FALSE)
        att.os <- ff.att.analysis(dataset, 
                                  outcome_type = "OS",
                                  method = causal_method,
                                  pred_vars = pred_vars,
                                  include_balance = FALSE)
      } else {
        att.dfs <- list(
          hr = mi_results$att.pooled.dfs,
          log_hr = log(mi_results$att.pooled.dfs),
          se = mi_results$att.se.dfs,
          pvalue = mi_results$att.pvalue.dfs,
          ci_lower = exp(mi_results$ci_lower_dfs),
          ci_upper = exp(mi_results$ci_upper_dfs)
        )
        att.os <- list(
          hr = mi_results$att.pooled.os,
          log_hr = log(mi_results$att.pooled.os),
          se = mi_results$att.se.os,
          pvalue = mi_results$att.pvalue.os,
          ci_lower = exp(mi_results$ci_lower_os),
          ci_upper = exp(mi_results$ci_upper_os)
        )
      }
    } else {
      stop(paste("ANALYZE ERROR: Unknown missing_method:", missing_method))
    }
    
    # Validate
    for(res in list(att.dfs, att.os)) {
      for(field in c("hr", "se", "pvalue", "ci_lower", "ci_upper")) {
        if(is.null(res[[field]]) || !is.finite(res[[field]]))
          stop(paste("Invalid", field, "in", label, "result"))
      }
      if(res$ci_lower <= 0 || res$ci_upper <= 0)
        stop(paste("CI must be positive (HR scale) in", label))
      if(res$ci_lower >= res$ci_upper)
        stop(paste("CI lower >= upper in", label))
    }
    
    list(dfs = att.dfs, os = att.os)
  }
  
  # Oracle always uses complete case
  oracle <- run_analysis(dat$dataset.complete, "oracle", force_complete_case = TRUE)
  
  # Missing datasets use whatever missing_method specifies
  if(in.mis.subj > 0) {
    res_mcar <- run_analysis(dat$dataset.mcar, "mcar")
    res_mar <- run_analysis(dat$dataset.mar,  "mar")
    res_mnar <- run_analysis(dat$dataset.mnar, "mnar")
  } else {
    res_mcar <- oracle
    res_mar <- oracle
    res_mnar <- oracle
  }
  
  # Package into single results row 
  data.frame(
    # Oracle (complete data) estimates
    oracle.hr.dfsb= oracle$dfs$hr,
    oracle.hr.os = oracle$os$hr,
    
    # MCAR estimates
    mcar.hr.dfs = res_mcar$dfs$hr,
    mcar.hr.os = res_mcar$os$hr,
    mcar.se.dfs = res_mcar$dfs$se,
    mcar.se.os = res_mcar$os$se,
    mcar.pvalue.dfs = res_mcar$dfs$pvalue,
    mcar.pvalue.os = res_mcar$os$pvalue,
    mcar.ci.lower.dfs = res_mcar$dfs$ci_lower,
    mcar.ci.upper.dfs = res_mcar$dfs$ci_upper,
    mcar.ci.lower.os = res_mcar$os$ci_lower,
    mcar.ci.upper.os = res_mcar$os$ci_upper,
    
    # MAR estimates
    mar.hr.dfs = res_mar$dfs$hr,
    mar.hr.os = res_mar$os$hr,
    mar.se.dfs = res_mar$dfs$se,
    mar.se.os = res_mar$os$se,
    mar.pvalue.dfs = res_mar$dfs$pvalue,
    mar.pvalue.os = res_mar$os$pvalue,
    mar.ci.lower.dfs = res_mar$dfs$ci_lower,
    mar.ci.upper.dfs = res_mar$dfs$ci_upper,
    mar.ci.lower.os = res_mar$os$ci_lower,
    mar.ci.upper.os = res_mar$os$ci_upper,
    
    # MNAR estimates
    mnar.hr.dfs = res_mnar$dfs$hr,
    mnar.hr.os = res_mnar$os$hr,
    mnar.se.dfs = res_mnar$dfs$se,
    mnar.se.os = res_mnar$os$se,
    mnar.pvalue.dfs = res_mnar$dfs$pvalue,
    mnar.pvalue.os = res_mnar$os$pvalue,
    mnar.ci.lower.dfs = res_mnar$dfs$ci_lower,
    mnar.ci.upper.dfs = res_mnar$dfs$ci_upper,
    mnar.ci.lower.os = res_mnar$os$ci_lower,
    mnar.ci.upper.os = res_mnar$os$ci_upper,
    
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}
```

The following is a 2nd version of the analysis function that is used with the 2n version of generate. For now it is not needed or used unless the first version somehow blows up. This version assumes Generate only makes the complete dataset and hence the missingness is done here instead. 
```{r analyze_v.2}
Analyze_WithEmbeddedBalance2 <- function(condition, dat, fixed_objects = NULL) {
  
  suppressPackageStartupMessages({
    library(survival); library(MASS)
    library(WeightIt); library(ebal)
    library(mice); library(dplyr)
  })
  
  # dat IS dataset.complete, passed directly from Generate 
  cat("CLASS OF DAT:", class(dat), 
      "| NROW:", nrow(dat), 
      "| NCOL:", ncol(dat), "\n")
  
  if(!is.data.frame(dat) || is.null(colnames(dat)) || nrow(dat) == 0)
    stop(paste("ANALYZE ERROR: dat is not a valid dataframe.",
               "Class:", class(dat), "Nrow:", nrow(dat)))
  
  pred_vars <- fixed_objects$pred.vars.processed
  cat_vars <- fixed_objects$cat.vars.processed
  cont_vars <- fixed_objects$cont.vars.processed
  causal_method <- condition$causal_method
  missing_method <- condition$missing_method
  in.mis.subj <- condition$input_mis_subj
  miss_vars <- condition$miss_vars
  if(is.list(miss_vars)) miss_vars <- unlist(miss_vars)
  
  # dat is always the complete dataset
  dataset.complete <- dat
  
  # Rebuild missing datasets inside Analyze
  # This avoids SimDesign list-unwrapping issues entirely
  if(in.mis.subj > 0) {
    
    dataset.mcar <- ff.miss.sub.mcar(
      dataset = dataset.complete,
      in.subj = in.mis.subj,
      in.pred = miss_vars
    )
    dataset.mar <- ff.miss.mar(
      dataset = dataset.complete,
      in.subj = in.mis.subj,
      in.pred = miss_vars,
      drivers = fixed_objects$mar_drivers[miss_vars]
    )
    dataset.mnar <- ff.miss.mnar(
      dataset = dataset.complete,
      in.subj = in.mis.subj,
      in.pred = miss_vars
    )
    
    # Validate missing datasets
    for(nm in c("dataset.mcar", "dataset.mar", "dataset.mnar")) {
      ds <- get(nm)
      if(is.null(colnames(ds)) || nrow(ds) == 0)
        stop(paste("ANALYZE ERROR:", nm, "invalid after missingness application"))
      cat(nm, "missingness applied — NAs in miss_vars:",
          sum(is.na(ds[, miss_vars])), "\n")
    }
    
  } else {
    dataset.mcar <- dataset.complete
    dataset.mar <- dataset.complete
    dataset.mnar <- dataset.complete
  }
  
  # Helper: run one analysis on one dataset
  run_analysis <- function(dataset, label) {
      cat("run_analysis called with label:", label, "\n")
    cat("dataset class:", class(dataset), "\n")
    cat("dataset nrow:", nrow(dataset), "\n")
    cat("dataset colnames:", paste(colnames(dataset), collapse = ", "), "\n")
    
    if(missing_method == "complete_case" || in.mis.subj == 0) {
      
      att.dfs <- ff.att.analysis(dataset, 
                                 outcome_type = "DFS",
                                 method = causal_method,
                                 pred_vars = pred_vars,
                                 include_balance = FALSE,
                                 include_diagnostics = FALSE)
      att.os  <- ff.att.analysis(dataset, 
                                 outcome_type = "OS",
                                 method = causal_method,
                                 pred_vars = pred_vars,
                                 include_balance = FALSE,
                                 include_diagnostics = FALSE)
      
    } else if(missing_method == "multiple_imputation") {
      
      nSim <- max(20, round(in.mis.subj * 100))
      
      mi_results <- enhanced_parallel_mi_analysis(
        dataset = dataset,
        pred_vars = pred_vars,
        cat_vars = cat_vars,
        cont_vars = cont_vars,
        causal_method = causal_method,
        in.mis.subj = in.mis.subj,
        nSim = nSim,
        sim_seed = as.integer(Sys.time()) + sample(1e6, 1)
      )
      
      if(is.null(mi_results)) {
        # No missingness found - fall back to complete case
        att.dfs <- ff.att.analysis(dataset, 
                                   outcome_type = "DFS",
                                   method = causal_method, 
                                   pred_vars = pred_vars,
                                   include_balance = FALSE)
        att.os <- ff.att.analysis(dataset, 
                                  outcome_type = "OS",
                                  method = causal_method, 
                                  pred_vars = pred_vars,
                                  include_balance = FALSE)
      } else {
        att.dfs <- list(
          hr = mi_results$att.pooled.dfs,
          log_hr = log(mi_results$att.pooled.dfs),
          se = mi_results$att.se.dfs,
          pvalue = mi_results$att.pvalue.dfs,
          ci_lower = exp(mi_results$ci_lower_dfs),
          ci_upper = exp(mi_results$ci_upper_dfs)
        )
        att.os <- list(
          hr = mi_results$att.pooled.os,
          log_hr = log(mi_results$att.pooled.os),
          se = mi_results$att.se.os,
          pvalue = mi_results$att.pvalue.os,
          ci_lower = exp(mi_results$ci_lower_os),
          ci_upper = exp(mi_results$ci_upper_os)
        )
      }
      
    } else {
      stop(paste("ANALYZE ERROR: Unknown missing_method:", missing_method))
    }
    
    # Validate
    for(res in list(att.dfs, att.os)) {
      for(field in c("hr", "se", "pvalue", "ci_lower", "ci_upper")) {
        if(is.null(res[[field]]) || !is.finite(res[[field]]))
          stop(paste("Invalid", field, "in", label, "result"))
      }
      if(res$ci_lower <= 0 || res$ci_upper <= 0)
        stop(paste("CI must be positive (HR scale) in", label))
      if(res$ci_lower >= res$ci_upper)
        stop(paste("CI lower >= upper in", label))
    }
    
    list(dfs = att.dfs, os = att.os)
  }

  # Oracle: run on complete data 
  oracle <- run_analysis(dataset.complete, "oracle")
  
  # Run on all three missing datasets 
  if(in.mis.subj > 0) {
    res_mcar <- run_analysis(dataset.mcar, "mcar")
    res_mar <- run_analysis(dataset.mar,  "mar")
    res_mnar <- run_analysis(dataset.mnar, "mnar")
  } else {
    res_mcar <- oracle
    res_mar <- oracle
    res_mnar <- oracle
  }
  
  # Package into single results row 
  data.frame(
    # Oracle (complete data) estimates
    oracle.hr.dfs = oracle$dfs$hr,
    oracle.hr.os = oracle$os$hr,
    
    # MCAR estimates
    mcar.hr.dfs = res_mcar$dfs$hr,
    mcar.hr.os = res_mcar$os$hr,
    mcar.se.dfs = res_mcar$dfs$se,
    mcar.se.os = res_mcar$os$se,
    mcar.pvalue.dfs = res_mcar$dfs$pvalue,
    mcar.pvalue.os = res_mcar$os$pvalue,
    mcar.ci.lower.dfs = res_mcar$dfs$ci_lower,
    mcar.ci.upper.dfs = res_mcar$dfs$ci_upper,
    mcar.ci.lower.os = res_mcar$os$ci_lower,
    mcar.ci.upper.os = res_mcar$os$ci_upper,
    
    # MAR estimates
    mar.hr.dfs = res_mar$dfs$hr,
    mar.hr.os = res_mar$os$hr,
    mar.se.dfs = res_mar$dfs$se,
    mar.se.os = res_mar$os$se,
    mar.pvalue.dfs = res_mar$dfs$pvalue,
    mar.pvalue.os = res_mar$os$pvalue,
    mar.ci.lower.dfs = res_mar$dfs$ci_lower,
    mar.ci.upper.dfs = res_mar$dfs$ci_upper,
    mar.ci.lower.os = res_mar$os$ci_lower,
    mar.ci.upper.os = res_mar$os$ci_upper,
    
    # MNAR estimates
    mnar.hr.dfs = res_mnar$dfs$hr,
    mnar.hr.os = res_mnar$os$hr,
    mnar.se.dfs = res_mnar$dfs$se,
    mnar.se.os = res_mnar$os$se,
    mnar.pvalue.dfs = res_mnar$dfs$pvalue,
    mnar.pvalue.os = res_mnar$os$pvalue,
    mnar.ci.lower.dfs = res_mnar$dfs$ci_lower,
    mnar.ci.upper.dfs = res_mnar$dfs$ci_upper,
    mnar.ci.lower.os = res_mnar$os$ci_lower,
    mnar.ci.upper.os = res_mnar$os$ci_upper,
    
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}
```

The final part of the DesignSim - the summarise function. This is pretty simple - it just creates the summary statistics we want for each simulation condition. This usually means just taking the mean/sd over the replicants. 
```{r, summarise_results}
Summarise <- function(condition, results, fixed_objects = NULL) {
  
  true_log_hr_dfs <- log(condition$input_hr_dfs)
  true_log_hr_os <- log(condition$input_hr_os)
  
  # Helper to compute all metrics for one mechanism
  compute_metrics <- function(hr_dfs, 
                              hr_os, 
                              ci_lower_dfs, 
                              ci_upper_dfs,
                              ci_lower_os,  
                              ci_upper_os,
                              pvalue_dfs,   
                              pvalue_os,
                              prefix) {
    
    bias_dfs <- log(hr_dfs) - true_log_hr_dfs
    bias_os <- log(hr_os) - true_log_hr_os
    
    setNames(data.frame(
      # HR estimates 
      mean(hr_dfs, na.rm = TRUE),
      median(hr_dfs, na.rm = TRUE),
      mean(hr_os, na.rm = TRUE),
      median(hr_os, na.rm = TRUE),
      
      # DFS metrics 
      mean(bias_dfs, na.rm = TRUE),
      sqrt(mean(bias_dfs^2, na.rm = TRUE)),
      mean(ci_lower_dfs <= condition$input_hr_dfs &
           ci_upper_dfs >= condition$input_hr_dfs, na.rm = TRUE),
      mean(pvalue_dfs < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE),
      
      # OS metrics 
      mean(bias_os, na.rm = TRUE),
      sqrt(mean(bias_os^2, na.rm = TRUE)),
      mean(ci_lower_os <= condition$input_hr_os &
           ci_upper_os >= condition$input_hr_os, na.rm = TRUE),
      mean(pvalue_os < 0.05,  na.rm = TRUE)
      
    ), paste0(prefix, c(
      ".mean.hr.dfs",   
      ".median.hr.dfs",
      ".mean.hr.os",    
      ".median.hr.os",
      ".bias.dfs",      
      ".rmse.dfs",
      ".coverage.dfs",  
      ".power.dfs",
      ".bias.os",       
      ".rmse.os",
      ".coverage.os",   
      ".power.os"
    )))
  }
  
  cbind(
    data.frame(
      true.hr.dfs = condition$input_hr_dfs,
      true.hr.os = condition$input_hr_os,
      n_replications = nrow(results)
    ),
    compute_metrics(
      results$oracle.hr.dfs, results$oracle.hr.os,
      results$mcar.ci.lower.dfs, results$mcar.ci.upper.dfs,
      results$mcar.ci.lower.os, results$mcar.ci.upper.os,
      results$mcar.pvalue.dfs, results$mcar.pvalue.os,
      prefix = "oracle"
    ),
    compute_metrics(
      results$mcar.hr.dfs, results$mcar.hr.os,
      results$mcar.ci.lower.dfs, results$mcar.ci.upper.dfs,
      results$mcar.ci.lower.os, results$mcar.ci.upper.os,
      results$mcar.pvalue.dfs, results$mcar.pvalue.os,
      prefix = "mcar"
    ),
    compute_metrics(
      results$mar.hr.dfs, results$mar.hr.os,
      results$mar.ci.lower.dfs, results$mar.ci.upper.dfs,
      results$mar.ci.lower.os, results$mar.ci.upper.os,
      results$mar.pvalue.dfs, results$mar.pvalue.os,
      prefix = "mar"
    ),
    compute_metrics(
      results$mnar.hr.dfs, results$mnar.hr.os,
      results$mnar.ci.lower.dfs, results$mnar.ci.upper.dfs,
      results$mnar.ci.lower.os, results$mnar.ci.upper.os,
      results$mnar.pvalue.dfs, results$mnar.pvalue.os,
      prefix = "mnar"
    )
  )
}
```

With this we are done with the simulation design code. All that is left is running the simulation on specificed conditions. This is given by the `Design` function. The tricky part is deciding what we want for our Design. 
```{r}
empirical_dist <- ff.mean.sigma(ref.data.processed)
baseline_models <- estimate_baseline_models(data)
treatment_models <- estimate_treatment_models(data)

# MAR drivers defined once here, passed to all replicates
mar_drivers <- list(
  "ENE_bin" = c("Total_LN_positive_", "pN_7th_bin"),
  "Total_LN_positive_" = c("Max_tumor_size", "pT_7th_bin"),
  "Age_Dx_bin" = c("pT_7th_bin", "PORT_bin"),
  "pN_7th_bin" = c("Total_LN_positive_", "Max_tumor_size"),
  "PORT_bin" = c("pN_7th_bin", "ENE_bin"),
  "Max_tumor_size" = c("pT_7th_bin", "pN_7th_bin"),
  "pT_7th_bin" = c("Max_tumor_size", "Age_Dx_bin")
)

# Fixed objects 
fixed_objects <- list(
  empirical_dist  = empirical_dist,
  baseline_models = baseline_models,
  treatment_models = treatment_models,
  pred.vars.processed = pred.vars.processed,
  cat.vars.processed  = cat.vars.processed,
  cont.vars.processed = cont.vars.processed,
  original_data = ref.data.processed,
  mar_drivers = mar_drivers
)

for(obj_name in names(fixed_objects)) {
  if(is.null(fixed_objects[[obj_name]])) {
    stop(paste("FIXED_OBJECTS ERROR:", obj_name, "is NULL"))
  }
}
cat("Fixed objects validated successfully\n")

############################## Simulation design ##############################
# missing_mechanism removed from condition grid — 
# Generate now produces all three missing datasets per replicate
# Analyze runs all three mechanisms in one pass
# This reduces conditions from 243 to 54 and ensures 
# MCAR/MAR/MNAR comparisons are within-replicate (same base dataset)

# An example of a final design might look like this:
Design <- createDesign(
  input_ss       = c(500, 1000, 2000),          # sample sizes
  input_hr_dfs   = c(1.0, 1.5, 2.5),            # DFS hazard ratios
  input_hr_os    = c(1.0, 1.3, 1.7),            # OS hazard ratios
  input_mis_subj = c(0, 0.25, 0.50),            # missingness proportions
  causal_method  = c("match", "ebal", "iptw"),     # causal methods
  missing_method = c("complete_case",
                     "multiple_imputation"),    # missing data analysis
  miss_vars      = list(
    c("ENE_bin", "Total_LN_positive_")),          # strongest confounders
  treatment_type = "engraftment"
)

if(is.null(Design) || nrow(Design) == 0) {
  stop("DESIGN ERROR: Failed to create simulation design matrix")
}
```

```{r}
# After preprocessing and model fitting — run this once
saveRDS(fixed_objects, "env/fixed_objects.rds")
saveRDS(empirical_dist, "env/empirical_dist.rds")
saveRDS(baseline_models, "env/baseline_models.rds")
saveRDS(treatment_models, "env/treatment_models.rds")
saveRDS(original_data, "env/original_data.rds")
saveRDS(Design, "env/Design.rds")
saveRDS(pred.vars.processed, "env/pred_vars.rds")
saveRDS(cat.vars.processed, "env/cat_vars.rds")
saveRDS(cont.vars.processed, "env/cont_vars.rds")

cat("All objects saved\n")
```


Before we run the entire simulation we can do a test run first. 

```{r}
# Test Generate on one condition
test_condition <- Design[1, ]

test_data <- Generate(test_condition, fixed_objects)

# Check all four datasets came back
names(test_data)  # should show complete, mcar, mar, mnar

# Check dimensions and missingness
nrow(test_data$dataset.complete)
colSums(is.na(test_data$dataset.mcar))
colSums(is.na(test_data$dataset.mar))
colSums(is.na(test_data$dataset.mnar))
```

######

Results for the poster. This section will the simulation study done for the poster (smaller subset of results). 

```{r}
Design_poster <- createDesign(
  input_ss       = 500,               
  input_hr_dfs   = c(1.0, 1.5, 2.0),       
  input_hr_os    = c(1.0, 1.5, 2.0),
  input_mis_subj = c(0, 0.25, 0.50),
  causal_method  = c("iptw", "ebal", "match"),
  missing_method = c("complete_case", "multiple_imputation"),
  miss_vars      = "ENE_bin, Total_LN_positive_",
  treatment_type = "engraftment"
)

cat("Total conditions:", nrow(Design_poster), "\n")
design_poster_cc <- Design_poster[
  Design_poster$missing_method == "complete_case", ]
design_poster_mi <- Design_poster[
  Design_poster$missing_method == "multiple_imputation" &
  Design_poster$input_mis_subj  > 0, ]

cat("CC conditions: ", nrow(design_poster_cc), "\n")  # 36
cat("MI conditions: ", nrow(design_poster_mi), "\n")  # 24
```

Once we have a design, the `runSimulation` function will do everything for us. Recommendtion: for larger cases it might be better to break up the simulation. For example, between CC and MI parts to speed up runtime and avoid any issues of crashing overnight.
```{r design_simulation_CC}
SimClean()
results_poster_cc <- runSimulation(
  design        = design_poster_cc,
  replications  = 200,
  generate      = Generate,
  analyse       = Analyze_WithEmbeddedBalance,
  summarise     = Summarise,
  fixed_objects = fixed_objects,
  parallel      = TRUE,
  ncores        = parallel::detectCores() - 1,
  save          = TRUE,
  save_results  = TRUE,
  filename      = "outputs/poster_cc",
  verbose       = TRUE
)

filename_final_poster_cc <- paste0("outputs/poster_cc.csv")
write.csv(results_poster_cc, filename_final_poster_cc)
```

```{r design_simulation_MI}
SimClean()
results_poster_mi <- runSimulation(
  design        = design_poster_mi,
  replications  = 200,             
  generate      = Generate,
  analyse       = Analyze_WithEmbeddedBalance,
  summarise     = Summarise,
  fixed_objects = fixed_objects,
  parallel      = TRUE,
  ncores        = parallel::detectCores() - 1,
  save          = TRUE,
  save_results  = TRUE,
  filename      = "outputs/poster_mi_long",
  verbose       = TRUE
)

filename_final_poster_mi <- paste0("outputs/poster_mi_long.csv")
write.csv(results_poster_mi_long, filename_final_poster_mi)
```

```{r design_simulation_FULL}
# Combine all
results_poster_full <- rbind(
  results_poster_cc,
  results_poster_mi
)
saveRDS(results_poster_full, "outputs/poster_full_final.rds")
filename_final_poster <- paste0("outputs/poster_data.csv")
write.csv(results_poster_full, filename_final_poster)
```


####
```{r}
#Save work to excel
timestamp <- format(Sys.time(), "%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")
filename <- paste0("outputs/pilot_oscc_simulation_", timestamp, ".xlsx")
write_xlsx(pilot_results, filename)
```


What the full simulation might look like. 
```{r}
#SimClean()
full_results <- runSimulation(
  design        = Design,
  replications  = 100,
  generate      = Generate,
  analyse       = Analyze_WithEmbeddedBalance,
  summarise     = Summarise,
  fixed_objects = fixed_objects,
  parallel      = TRUE,
  ncores        = parallel::detectCores() - 1,
  save          = TRUE,
  save_results  = TRUE,
  filename      = "full_simulation_results",
  verbose       = TRUE
)
```


```{r}
#Save work to excel
# simulation broke
timestamp <- format(Sys.time(), "%Y%m%d_%H%M%S")
filename_final <- paste0("outputs/full_oscc_simulation_", timestamp, ".xlsx")
write_xlsx(full_simulation_results, filename_final)
```
